key_insight: An adaptive multiscale P-IMPES scheme updates coarse multiscale spaces only when saturation-driven permeability changes are significant, preserving physics while reducing unnecessary recomputation.
engineering_relevance: The method enables accurate, locally conservative two-phase flow simulation in high-contrast porous media, which is useful for reservoir and subsurface transport modeling.
method: Couples physics-preserving implicit-pressure explicit-saturation (P-IMPES) with mixed constraint energy minimizing GMsFEM.
Monitors mobility-weighted coefficient changes and regenerates multiscale basis functions only when a tolerance is exceeded.
Applies local postprocessing to recover fine-grid mass conservation and preserve phase-wise physical constraints.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical guarantees plus numerical experiments on multiple high-contrast permeability fields showing that smaller adaptive tolerances improve saturation accuracy while avoiding unnecessary multiscale updates.
watch_item: Performance depends on selecting an appropriate adaptive tolerance and CFL condition; overly aggressive updating or violation of bounds can reduce stability and efficiency.
paper_title: An Adaptive and Physics-Preserving Multiscale Method for Two-Phase Flow Simulations in High-Contrast Heterogeneous Porous Media
